question: Q-open-composition-kernel-closure
hypothesis: A fixed-depth-free OpenHSWM value is closed under finite composition; regrouping normalizes to one digest; compose preserves mount, port, connector, weight, provenance, and identity state without materializing a legacy Field.
falsifiers: A composite cannot be composed again through the same OpenHSWM API.
compose invokes or returns the legacy materialized Field.
Two mount ids for one field digest collapse to one mount.
A connector can address a non-exported or missing port.
NaN, infinity, or positive log-salience is accepted.
Separation plus recomposition does not recover the source digest.
Unsupported n-ary materialization silently loses endpoints.
Existing B0 field algebra tests regress.
non_claims: No learned routing or agent policy is implemented.
No Lakatos progressive verdict may be inferred from property-test success.
